text.title: FinOps — Cloud Cost Allocation via Tagging Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in production. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: FinOps spends 3 weeks tagging 4,000 legacy resources; one quarter later 1,800 of them have drifted (recreated, renamed, deleted).
antipattern.root-cause: Allocation problem treated as a one-time backfill, not a creation-time policy.
antipattern.fix: Add SCP / Org Policy `deny` on resource creation without required tags; re-run the backfill once; the steady state is then maintained automatically.

antipattern.symptom: Cost report shows 25 different spellings of 'platform' team; allocation totals don't reconcile to budgets.
antipattern.root-cause: No controlled vocabulary; tag value is free-text.
antipattern.fix: Define a controlled vocabulary in the SCP (`tag:team` ∈ {platform, billing, analytics, ...}); reject creates outside the set.

antipattern.symptom: Tags exist on every resource but billing CSV has no `team` column; report tool can't allocate.
antipattern.root-cause: Author tagged resources but never activated keys in billing console.
antipattern.fix: Mark required tag keys as 'cost allocation tags' in billing console; wait 24h for backfill; verify in CUR / BigQuery export.
